page: Alibaba Cloud ecs.c8ine.large is an x86_64 architecture server instance powered by an Intel Xeon Sapphire Rapids processor. It features 2 dedicated physical cores with 1 thread per core, operating at a clock speed of 2.7 GHz. The instance is provisioned with 4.0 GB of system memory, yielding a memory-to-core ratio of 2.0 GB per core.

This instance utilizes the KVM hypervisor and does not include local storage, requiring external block storage volumes for persistent data. Network connectivity is defined by a baseline bandwidth of 4 Gbps. No complimentary public IPv4 addresses are bundled with this server type.

The 1:2 vCPU-to-RAM ratio positions this instance in the compute-optimized category, offering dedicated CPU resources without hyperthreading. This design ensures predictable compute performance but limits memory-intensive operations. It is qualitatively cost-efficient for workloads requiring dedicated CPU execution with low memory footprints, such as lightweight web serving, microservices, and basic CI/CD tasks.
description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.c8ine.large is a compute-optimized instance built on the x86_64 architecture and powered by an Intel Xeon Sapphire Rapids processor. It features 2 dedicated physical cores running at 2.7 GHz with 1 thread per core, ensuring predictable compute performance without hyperthreading. The instance is equipped with 4.0 GB of RAM, providing a 2.0 GB per core memory allocation. It operates on the KVM hypervisor and lacks local storage, relying entirely on network-attached storage. Network performance is characterized by a 4 Gbps baseline bandwidth. With its 1:2 vCPU-to-RAM ratio and dedicated CPU allocation, this instance is designed for workloads that require consistent CPU performance with minimal memory requirements. It is suitable for lightweight web serving, microservices, and small-scale CI/CD pipelines.
